page: Amazon Web Services m6id.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server powered by dual Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features 128 vCPUs, representing 64 physical cores with hyper-threading, running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. This processing power is paired with 512 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The physical hardware configuration includes 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile characterized by strong multi-core capabilities and moderate single-core speeds. Multi-core operations are highly competitive, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Passmark CPU scores, and Geekbench ray tracing and image processing all ranking in the top tier (top 10%). Conversely, single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average (middle 50%) category. Memory bandwidth is strong, placing in the top 25% for both cached small-block and uncached large-block reads.

This bare-metal server offers high resource density, combining substantial memory, high-speed local NVMe storage, and high-core-count dedicated compute. This configuration supports multi-threaded workloads, database operations, and large-scale web serving. Passmark database operations and Redis SET operations both register in the top 25%, indicating strong performance for data-intensive applications. However, for workloads reliant on single-threaded speed or advanced matrix extensions, the hardware presents a tradeoff due to its average single-core performance and lack of AMX instruction support.
description: Amazon Web Services m6id.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server configured with 128 vCPUs from Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz, 512 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier (top 10%) multi-core CPU capabilities, as demonstrated by stress-ng and Passmark CPU scores, alongside strong (top 25%) memory bandwidth and database operation throughput. However, single-core performance remains in the average tier. This resource-dense profile supports multi-threaded applications, large-scale databases, and high-throughput web serving where local high-speed storage and high-core-count parallel processing are required.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and 7600 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and strong database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 7600 GB NVMe SSD storage for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal platform combining 128 dedicated vCPUs with 7.6 TB of local NVMe storage for demanding multi-threaded and database workloads.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors at 3.5 GHz
512 GB of DDR4 system memory paired with 50 Gbps baseline networking
7600 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD instance storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU, ray tracing, and image processing performance
Average single-core performance represents a tradeoff for single-threaded tasks
Supports large-scale database operations and multi-threaded applications
